gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A definition's **declared** effect set (§4.5 I3) — what it says it can do, on its signature."] pub type EffectSet = BTreeSet < EffectKind > ;
gaps.reason: `type EffectSet = ...` alias — Mycelium's `type_item` always introduces a new nominal sum type via `'=' constructor ('|' constructor)*`; a bare alias to an existing type would fabricate a sum type where none exists semantically

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An effect a definition performs but did **not** declare (I3) — an explicit checker error, never"] # [doc = " silent. This is the \"no unknown side effects\" guarantee. A *checker* concern (tooling layer), not a"] # [doc = " runtime one, so it stays here rather than in the shared `mycelium-interp` budget primitive (KC-3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct UndeclaredEffect { # [doc = " The performed-but-undeclared effect."] pub effect : EffectKind , }
gaps.reason: struct `UndeclaredEffect` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet:
impl fmt :: Display for UndeclaredEffect { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { write ! (f , "undeclared effect {:?}: a definition may not perform an effect absent from its signature \
             (RFC-0014 §4.5 I3 — no unknown side effects); declare it" , self . effect . to_string ()) } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **compositional no-undeclared-effect check** (I3): every effect a definition *performs* (its own"] # [doc = " plus its callees', composed up the call graph) must be in its *declared* set. Returns the first"] # [doc = " undeclared effect, if any. This *checks* declared effects compose — it never *infers* one (an effect"] # [doc = " can never become implicit; §8 resolved: manual-declare + compositional-check)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`UndeclaredEffect`] for the first performed effect not in `declared`."] pub fn check_effects (declared : & EffectSet , performed : & EffectSet) -> Result < () , UndeclaredEffect > { for e in performed { if ! declared . contains (e) { return Err (UndeclaredEffect { effect : e . clone () }) ; } } Ok (()) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [test] fn the_ledger_primitive_is_the_shared_interp_type () { let mut b = Budgets :: new () . with (EffectBudget :: Attempts (1)) ; assert ! (b . consume (EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . is_ok ()) ; let exhausted = b . consume (EffectKind :: Retry , 1) . unwrap_err () ; let as_eval : mycelium_interp :: EvalError = exhausted . into () ; assert ! (matches ! (as_eval , mycelium_interp :: EvalError :: EffectBudget (_))) ; } # [test] fn an_undeclared_effect_is_an_explicit_checker_error () { let declared : EffectSet = [EffectKind :: Alloc] . into_iter () . collect () ; let performed : EffectSet = [EffectKind :: Alloc , EffectKind :: Io] . into_iter () . collect () ; let err = check_effects (& declared , & performed) . unwrap_err () ; assert_eq ! (err . effect , EffectKind :: Io) ; } }
